entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Late photoperiod-sensitive tropical sorghum varieties used in the study

entities.name: DELAYED FLOWERING
entities.description: A pronounced delay in the onset of flowering observed in sorghum sown in winter

entities.name: PROSTRATE HIGH-TILLERING FORMS
entities.description: Modified morphogenesis in sorghum characterized by prostrate growth and high tillering

entities.name: SHORT-DAY CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions with short day lengths prevailing during winter months

entities.name: WINTER MONTHS
entities.description: The period from November to January in the northern hemisphere used for sowing

entities.name: NORTHERN HEMISPHERE
entities.description: The geographic region where the sowing occurred during winter months

entities.name: LOW NIGHT TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Low temperatures during the night, initially hypothesized to cause delayed flowering

entities.name: MONTHLY SOWINGS
entities.description: The experimental procedure of sowing sorghum every month from December to March

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: Controlled environment used to test the effects of temperature and UV radiation

entities.name: FIELD
entities.description: Natural outdoor environment used to compare plant growth with greenhouse conditions

entities.name: SOIL CONDITIONS
entities.description: The physical and chemical properties of the soil in the field

entities.name: BAMAKO, MALI
entities.description: The specific geographic location where the research was conducted

entities.name: PANICLE INITIATION DATE
entities.description: The specific date when the panicle begins to initiate in the sorghum plant

entities.name: LEAF PRIMORDIA INITIATION RATE
entities.description: The rate at which leaf primordia are initiated at the stem apex

entities.name: LEAF APPEARANCE RATE
entities.description: The rate at which leaves appear on the sorghum plant

entities.name: VEGETATIVE-PHASE DURATION
entities.description: The length of the vegetative growth period before flowering

entities.name: THERMAL TIME
entities.description: A cumulative measure of heat used to quantify plant development

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The nutrient richness of the potting soil compared to natural field soil

entities.name: UV RADIATION
entities.description: The ultraviolet component of sunlight, suggested as a mediator for the flowering cue

entities.name: SUNRISE AND SUNSET HOUR
entities.description: The daily changes in the timing of sunrise and sunset, suggested as the cue for delayed flowering

entities.name: SMALL-GRAIN CEREALS
entities.description: Winter cereals that exhibit rosette growth forms

entities.name: ROSETTES
entities.description: A growth form characterized by a cluster of leaves close to the ground
